skill.name: gsap
skill.description: GSAP animation reference for HyperFrames. Covers gsap.to(), from(), fromTo(), easing, stagger, defaults, timelines (gsap.timeline(), position parameter, labels, nesting, playback), and performance (transforms, will-change, quickTo). Use when writing GSAP animations in HyperFrames compositions.
skill.summary: GSAP animation reference for HyperFrames with drop-in effects for video compositions.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 171 potential issues, but evaluation determined all critical and high findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified HTML script tags as shell commands, FFT signal processing as weak cryptography, and generic variable names as credential access patterns. The Python audio extraction script uses subprocess to run ffmpeg for legitimate audio processing. No malicious behavior or user input injection vectors detected.

high_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
high_findings.description: The static scanner flagged np.fft.rfft() and related operations as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. This is incorrect - these are standard Fast Fourier Transform operations used for audio signal processing, not cryptography. FFT decomposes audio signals into frequency components for visualization.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading the actual file - np.fft.rfft() is numpy's FFT function for audio frequency analysis, not encryption. The variable names and context clearly show audio processing workflow.

high_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positive: Credential Access Patterns
high_findings.description: The scanner flagged variable names like 'samples', 'SAMPLE_RATE', and file references as 'Windows SAM database' access. This is pattern-matching noise - the skill has no access to Windows credentials. Variables are standard Python naming for audio sample data.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Read the actual file - no Windows credential access code exists. Variables named 'samples' and 'SAMPLE_RATE' are standard audio processing nomenclature, not security-related.

medium_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positive: Ruby/Shell Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 139 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in references/effects.md and SKILL.md. These are false positives - the scanner detected HTML script tags like '<script src="https://...">' and markdown code snippets as shell commands. These are legitimate CDN URL references and documentation code examples.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading files - HTML <script src> tags and markdown code blocks are not shell commands. Scanner misidentified HTML markup as code execution patterns.

low_findings.title: Python subprocess.run for FFmpeg
low_findings.description: The extract-audio-data.py script uses subprocess.run() to invoke ffmpeg for audio decoding. This is a standard audio processing pattern with no injection risk - the command arguments are hardcoded except for the input path from command-line arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run is used correctly with hardcoded command and validated input path argument. No shell injection vector exists.

low_findings.title: XMLHttpRequest Usage for Audio Data Loading
low_findings.description: The audio visualizer effect uses synchronous XMLHttpRequest to load audio data files. This is documented as intentional for HyperFrames deterministic timeline construction. No user-controlled input or security vulnerability.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Synchronous XHR is intentional design pattern documented in effects.md for deterministic timeline construction. Loading local asset files, not user input.

content.user_title: Create professional GSAP animations for video
content.value_statement: Writing GSAP animations for video compositions requires precise timing and deterministic execution. This skill provides comprehensive GSAP documentation and drop-in effects for HyperFrames, enabling you to create smooth, professional animations without searching through multiple resources.
actual_capabilities: Create smooth tweens with gsap.to(), gsap.from(), and gsap.fromTo() with proper easing
Build complex timeline sequences with labels, nesting, and position parameters
Implement typewriter text effects using GSAP TextPlugin
Create audio visualizer animations driven by pre-extracted frequency data
Optimize animation performance using transform properties and quickTo
Handle responsive animations with gsap.matchMedia() for accessibility
limitations: Requires pre-extracted audio data for visualizer effects (run extract-audio-data.py)
Cannot animate layout properties (width, height) without performance cost
TextPlugin required for typewriter effect - not included in base GSAP

use_cases.title: Add entrance animations to video overlays
use_cases.description: Create fade-in, slide, or scale entrance animations for text overlays and UI elements in video compositions.
use_cases.target_user: Video content creators adding motion to overlays

use_cases.title: Build audio-reactive visualizers
use_cases.description: Create canvas-based audio visualizers that respond to music or speech in video backgrounds.
use_cases.target_user: Motion designers creating music-driven video content

use_cases.title: Sequence complex multi-step animations
use_cases.description: Coordinate multiple animations with precise timing using GSAP timelines, labels, and position parameters.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building animated landing pages exported to video

prompt_templates.title: Basic tween animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GSAP to animate #hero element fading in and sliding up 100px. Duration 0.6s with power2.out easing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple entrance animation for a heading

prompt_templates.title: Timeline sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GSAP timeline that: 1) fades in .title, 2) fades in .subtitle 0.2s after, 3) scales in .button at 0.5s. Use position parameters, not delay.

prompt_templates.title: Typewriter effect
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a typewriter animation for #message that types at 10 characters per second with a blinking cursor. Cursor should blink when idle.
prompt_templates.scenario: Text reveal animation for video narration

prompt_templates.title: Audio visualizer frame
prompt_templates.prompt: Render audio frame [FRAME] with bars using AUDIO_DATA.frames[0].bands array. Each band maps to a canvas rectangle. Bass drives scale, treble drives opacity.

output_examples.input: Animate .card elements with a stagger effect, 0.1s delay between each card, sliding in from the left
Build a timeline where .logo fades in first, then .headline slides up, then .subtitle fades in after headline completes
Type 'Welcome' at 12 characters per second with cursor '|' that blinks between solid and blinking states

best_practices: Use transform properties (x, y, scale, rotation) instead of layout properties for GPU acceleration
Pass defaults to timeline constructor rather than chaining with delay for readability
Store tween and timeline return values when you need to control playback programmatically
anti_patterns: Do not animate width, height, top, or left when transforms can achieve the same effect
Do not chain tweens with delay when a timeline with position parameters sequences them better
Do not create tweens targeting elements that do not yet exist in the DOM

faq.question: What is GSAP?
faq.answer: GSAP (GreenSock Animation Platform) is a JavaScript library for high-performance animations. It provides precise control over timing, easing, and sequencing that CSS animations cannot match.

faq.question: How do I create a timeline?
faq.answer: Use gsap.timeline() to create a timeline, then chain tweens with .to() or .from(). Tweens are added sequentially by default, or use position parameters like '<' or '+=0.5' for overlap.

faq.question: What is the position parameter?
faq.answer: The third argument in a tween controls its placement in the timeline. Use numbers for absolute time, '+=0.5' for relative to end, '<' for same start as previous, or a label name.

faq.question: How do typewriter effects work?
faq.answer: GSAP TextPlugin animates text content character by character. Set the duration based on text length divided by characters per second. Manage cursor state separately with class changes.

faq.question: Why use synchronous XHR for audio data?
faq.answer: HyperFrames reads window.__timelines synchronously after page load. Async data loading means the timeline is not ready when capture starts. This is intentional for deterministic video rendering.

faq.question: What audio formats does extract-audio-data.py support?
faq.answer: The script uses ffmpeg for decoding, so it supports any audio format ffmpeg handles: MP3, WAV, AAC, OGG, and video files containing audio (MP4, WebM, MOV).
